opp.name: Intuit ANA+P2P
opp.next_step: 6.29.26 KK - fu and had bdr call to connect for any updates 6.16.26 KK - invited to palo alto event and asked for time to connect 6.10.26 KK - introduced myself and asked for time 5.25.26 KC: Submitted draft commercial proposal and differentiators 5.8.26 KC: Respond with commercial proposal pricing estimates by 5/12 4.20.26 KC: Demo set for 4.24 to cover: end-to-end workflow for initiating Purchase req and converting it into a PO
opp.ais_why:
• Intuit Director of Sourcing Thornton James is exploring autonomous negotiations and AI-driven P2P efficiency, with growing company-wide investment in AI solutions across LOBs.
• Demo covered end-to-end PR-to-PO workflow and the team is now waiting on commercial pricing, with Coupa renewal opening a real displacement window this summer.
• Active Qualified deal with last activity May 8 and a clear commercial path forward, signaling sustained AI-led momentum.

sources: Avoma 31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026: notetaker removed at buyer request, no discovery content captured
Avoma 24 Apr 2026: demo aborted, Thornton no-show
Next_Step__c: no business-case or ROI mention
metrics.narrative: No quantified business case, ROI, or operational impact captured across four touchpoints. The notetaker was removed at buyer request on three of four calls (31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026), preventing discovery content capture. The 24 Apr demo was aborted (Thornton no-show). No pain points, savings targets, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, compliance lift, or automation value articulated by the buyer. Without a quantified value case Zycus cannot anchor pricing, build EB sponsorship, or defend against a lower-cost competitor. The ranked move is to run a structured discovery session with Thornton and his stakeholders to quantify pain and build the ROI model, then present it to the Economic Buyer.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Thornton James, Evaluator role, sole engaged contact
Avoma 31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026: Thornton sole buyer participant, notetaker removed on three calls
Next_Step__c 10 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026: rep outreach, no buyer reply
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Thornton James (Staff Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) is the sole engaged contact but shows no evidence of advocacy, no EB/DM access facilitation, no internal selling, and is now cooling (no reply to rep outreach 10 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun 2026). A true champion would introduce Zycus to Emma Chontos (CPO) or Kelly Mathis (Director), share internal docs, push the evaluation forward, and counter competitor narratives. Thornton requested notetaker removal on three calls, limiting Zycus visibility into his sentiment and blockers. The last buyer touch was 8 May (52 days ago); subsequent rep outreach has gone unanswered. For a $275k enterprise deal 53 days from close with no champion, no EB access, and a cooling sole contact, Zycus is politically exposed. If a competitor has secured a champion with EB access, Zycus is losing invisibly. The ranked move is to re-engage Thornton with a high-value offer (ROI workshop, exec briefing, peer reference) and request introduction to Emma Chontos and Kelly Mathis to build multi-threaded champion relationships.

sources: SF Competitors__c: blank
Avoma 31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026: no competitor mention captured (notetaker removed)
Next_Step__c: no competitor or RFP reference
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape unknown. No competitor named in Competitors__c, no RFP shortlist disclosed, no incumbent vendor identified, no multi-vendor bake-off signal, no buyer mention of alternatives across four touchpoints. For a $275k Fortune 500 procurement transformation deal (Intuit, ANA+P2P scope) at Formal Evaluation stage, the probability of a silent multi-vendor evaluation is near-certain. Competitors likely include SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, or Zip, and potentially an incumbent P2P/eProcurement vendor being displaced or extended. The notetaker-removal pattern (three of four calls) may indicate the buyer is withholding competitive intel. Without competitive positioning, Zycus is operating blind—differentiation, pricing strategy, and champion-building are guesses. High risk of losing to a better-positioned, better-connected rival. The ranked move is to ask Thornton directly on the next call who else is being evaluated, what the shortlist looks like, and how Zycus compares, and to validate with the broader buying committee.

sources: Avoma 31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026: notetaker removed, no pain captured
Avoma 24 Apr 2026: demo aborted
Products: ANA, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI imply broad scope, but buyer pain not articulated
identify_pain.narrative: No operational or financial pain articulated by the buyer across four touchpoints. The notetaker was removed on three calls (31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026), and the 24 Apr demo was aborted. No discussion of current-state procurement/P2P inefficiency, compliance gaps, manual processes, supplier onboarding friction, invoice cycle time, spend visibility, or automation opportunity captured. The deal scope (ANA, eProcurement, eInvoicing, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI) implies a broad procurement transformation, but the buyer has not stated the pain driving it, the cost of inaction, or the quantified impact. Without confirmed pain Zycus cannot build urgency, defend pricing, or anchor the Economic Buyer conversation. The ranked move is to run a pain-discovery session with Thornton and the broader procurement team to surface and quantify the operational/financial drivers.

sources: Avoma 8 May 2026: 'Commercial Discussion' call, notetaker removed, no content captured
Next_Step__c 25 May 2026: draft commercial proposal and differentiators submitted, but no contracting or legal next step
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics, MSA/SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec process, or implementation scoping discussed. The 8 May 2026 'Zycus and Intuit Commercial Discussion' call title suggests a commercial conversation was planned, but notetaker was removed immediately and no content was captured. No partner or SI involvement mentioned, no ERP/systems integration requirements, no security review timeline, no contract redline or approval process. With 53 days to close and no paper-process visibility, the path to signature is undefined. The ranked move is to surface the contracting and InfoSec process with Thornton and the procurement team, and to initiate MSA/SOW review if not already in motion.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Emma Chontos, Decision Maker role
Avoma 31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026: Emma Chontos absent from all four touchpoints
Next_Step__c 29 Jun 2026: rep outreach only, no EB engagement
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer identified as Emma Chontos, Head of Global Business Services & Chief Procurement Officer, via OpportunityContactRole (Decision Maker). Emma has not engaged across four touchpoints and 90 days (deal created 20 Mar 2026, last call 8 May 2026). No budget conversation, no approval-chain mapping, no executive sponsorship, no sign-off authority confirmed. The 8 May 2026 commercial discussion call had notetaker removed immediately; no EB participation and no commercial or pricing conversation captured. For a $275k Fortune 500 procurement deal at Formal Evaluation stage 53 days from close, zero EB engagement is a forecast-breaking vulnerability. The ranked move is for the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley, given as ground truth) to execute an executive-to-executive connect with the deal owner's manager to validate budget authority, secure active sponsorship, and map the approval chain.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: 7 contacts spanning CPO to Evaluator, but only Thornton engaged
Avoma 31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026: Thornton James sole buyer participant
Avoma 24 Apr 2026: Thornton no-show, demo aborted
decision_process.narrative: The call sequence shows an evaluator-level engagement pattern with no escalation. Thornton James (Staff Strategic Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) is the sole participant across all four touchpoints (31 Mar, 24 Apr aborted, 7 May, 8 May 2026). No senior stakeholders (Emma Chontos CPO, Kelly Mathis Director, Jason Demello Digital Procurement Leader, Johan Johansson Strategic Sourcing Leader) have joined. No approval chain, no procurement channel (direct vs partner), no InfoSec or legal gating, no contracting timeline discussed. The absence of escalation and multi-threading 90 days into the deal and 53 days from close signals either a low-priority evaluation or a process Zycus is not controlling. The ranked move is to request Thornton introduce Zycus to the broader buying committee and to map the formal approval and procurement process in writing.

sources: Next_Step__c 20 Apr 2026: demo planned for 24 Apr to cover Purchase req to PO workflow
Avoma 24 Apr 2026: demo aborted, Thornton no-show
Avoma 7 May, 8 May 2026: notetaker removed, no criteria captured
decision_criteria.narrative: No evaluation criteria, use-case requirements, RFP scope, BRD fit, test-case results, or workshop feedback captured. The 24 Apr 2026 demo was scheduled to cover 'end-to-end workflow for initiating Purchase req and converting it into a PO' but was aborted when Thornton did not join. Subsequent calls on 7 May and 8 May had notetaker removed at buyer request, preventing content capture. No structured discovery session, no use-case validation, no sourcing/procurement workflow requirements, no AI/Merlin appetite signal, no compliance or integration criteria. Without confirmed criteria Zycus cannot position differentiation, validate fit, or counter competitive alternatives. The ranked move is to run a structured use-case demo and BRD fit session with Thornton and the broader buying committee (Jason Demello, Johan Johansson, Siddharth Venugopal, Dapo Adegeye) to capture and validate decision criteria, then document them.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 20 Mar 2026 (101 days ago). Four buyer touchpoints Mar-May 2026: 31 Mar follow-up discussion, 24 Apr demo aborted (Thornton no-show), 7 May rescheduled demo (notetaker removed), 8 May commercial discussion (notetaker removed). Draft commercial proposal and differentiators submitted 25 May 2026. Buyer silent since 8 May (52 days). Rep (Karson Keogh) outreach 10 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun with no buyer reply. No discovery depth, no EB engagement, no multi-threading, no champion development, no competitive intel, no contracting prep. Deal is stalled at evaluator level with 53 days to close.

items.detail: Economic Buyer Emma Chontos (CPO) and Decision Maker Kelly Mathis (Director) have not engaged across four touchpoints and 90 days. No EB relationship, no budget/approval-chain mapping, no exec sponsorship. Single-threaded to Evaluator-level contact (Thornton James) who is cooling. 53 days to close with no exec alignment is a forecast-breaking vulnerability.

Close date 21 Aug 2026 (56 days out) is indefensible given current state: no champion, no EB engagement, no MEDDPICC depth, no decision criteria, no paper-process mapping, and 49 days since last buyer touch (8 May 2026). Stage (Formal Evaluation) requires deeper discovery, use-case demos, BRD fit, ROI workshop, multi-threading, pricing finalization, and contracting. Current pace cannot complete these gates by close date. Date will slip or deal will stall.

Differentiation and positioning strategy are guesses without competitive intel. High risk of losing to an invisible, better-positioned rival.

Last buyer engagement 8 May 2026 (52 days ago, commercial discussion call). Rep-initiated outreach 10 Jun, 16 Jun, 29 Jun with no buyer reply. north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded to cooling evaluator contact with no EB/DM engagement across 90 days; 53 days to close with no champion, no MEDDPICC depth, no competitive intel, and buyer silent since 8 May (52 days ago). Rep outreach 10/16/29 Jun awaiting reply.

flags: Single-threaded to Thornton James (Evaluator) who is cooling (no reply since 8 May, 52 days). Emma Chontos (CPO) and Kelly Mathis (Director) have not engaged. Multi-thread immediately to senior stakeholders (Jason Demello, Johan Johansson, Siddharth Venugopal) and secure EB access.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is single-threaded to a cooling evaluator-level contact with no EB/DM engagement, no champion, no competitive intel, and no MEDDPICC depth 53 days from close. Immediate re-engagement and multi-threading required.

competitive_position.summary: Competitive landscape unknown. No competitor named in Salesforce (Competitors__c null), no rival mentioned in available Avoma notes (31 Mar, 7 May, 8 May 2026), and Q3 competition-synthesis fields unread. No intel on whether Intuit is running a multi-vendor RFP, replacing an incumbent, or sole-sourcing Zycus. For a $275k enterprise procurement deal at a Fortune 500 company (Intuit), the probability of a silent bake-off against Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, or Jaggaer is high, but unconfirmed. Without competitive transparency, positioning and differentiation strategy are guesses. Immediate action required to surface the shortlist and scorecard within 7 days.

deltas.subject: Single-threaded to Thornton James (Evaluator) who is cooling (no reply since 8 May, 52 days). Emma Chontos (CPO) and Kelly Mathis (Director) have not engaged. Multi-thread immediately to senior stakeholders (Jason Demello, Johan Johansson, Siddharth Venugopal) and secure EB access.

deltas.to: No champion, no internal advocate. Thornton shows no advocacy evidence. For a $275k enterprise deal 53 days from close, champion-building is urgent. Offer high-
deltas.subject: No champion, no internal advocate. Thornton shows no advocacy evidence. For a $275k enterprise deal 53 days from close, champion-building is urgent. Offer high-value engagement (ROI workshop, exec briefing, peer reference) to develop Thornton or identify another champion with EB access.

deltas.to: Competitive landscape unknown (no competitor named, no RFP shortlist). For a Fortune 500 procurement deal, silent competition is near-certain. Run competitive d
deltas.subject: Competitive landscape unknown (no competitor named, no RFP shortlist). For a Fortune 500 procurement deal, silent competition is near-certain. Run competitive discovery with Thornton and the buying committee to surface the shortlist, position differentiation, and validate Zycus standing.

Champion void is the single highest win-probability risk: Thornton James (Staff Sourcing Manager, Evaluator role) is passive and has provided no competitive intel, no EB introduction, and no internal advocacy across four touchpoints and 87 days. For a $275k enterprise deal at Intuit (Fortune 500), an Evaluator-level single-thread is a losing position. ACTIVATE A CHAMPION within 7 days: offer Thornton a procurement-best-practice asset (e.g. peer-CIO Merlin AI case study, savings benchmark report) that raises his internal credibility, then use that goodwill to secure a three-way introduction to Emma Chontos (CPO) and Kelly Mathis (Global Procurement Director). Position the meeting as an exec-level ROI and AI-strategy discussion (not a vendor pitch), and use it to identify a true champion (someone with EB access who will coach us and sell for us internally). Without a champion, this deal cannot close by 21 Aug 2026.

Economic Buyer ghost is a forecast-killer: Emma Chontos (CPO, the org-chart EB for $275k procurement spend) has not appeared on any call or engaged with the draft proposal submitted 25 May 2026 (32 days ago). No budget, no approval-chain mapping, no commercial sign-off mechanism visible. At 56 days to close, the EB must be in active commercial conversation or the forecast is aspirational. SECURE DIRECT EB ENGAGEMENT within 7 days: the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley, given as ground truth) should reach out to Emma Chontos exec-to-exec, positioning a 30-minute ROI and Merlin-AI-strategy discussion (not a sales call) anchored to Intuit's digital-procurement transformation and peer-CIO success stories. Use the meeting to surface the budget holder, approval chain (CFO, other execs), decision timeline, and competitive shortlist, then convert Emma to active sponsor or disqualify the deal honestly.

Competitive darkness is a silent killer: 87 days in, no competitor named, no RFP shortlist intel, no incumbent-replacement context captured. For a Fortune 500 procurement deal this size, the probability of a silent multi-vendor bake-off (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer favored by enterprise procurement) is near-certain, yet Zycus is flying blind. Without knowing the competitive set and Intuit's internal scorecard, differentiation strategy and positioning are guesses. FORCE COMPETITIVE TRANSPARENCY within 7 days: in the champion-activation and EB-engagement calls, ask directly, 'Who else is Intuit evaluating alongside Zycus, and how are we positioned on your scorecard?' If buyer deflects, offer a peer-reference call with a named CIO who chose Zycus over and use that conversation to extract the shortlist. Then rebuild the narrative around the wedge (e.g. Merlin agentic AI vs Coupa's static workflows, total-cost-of-ownership vs Ariba's license creep, AI-hungry positioning vs legacy-vendor inertia).
